Ancient History and World Heritage Sites

Ajloun Islamic Castle, 'Ikremah Bin Abi Jahl Tomb -Amman","day_summary":"

After breakfast at your hotel in Amman, embark on a scenic drive north toward Ajloun, passing through lush pine forests and olive groves. Visit the impressive Ajloun Castle, a 12th-century fortress built by Usama bin Munqith, nephew of Saladin, to defend against Crusader attacks. Admire its Islamic architecture, towers, and panoramic hilltop views. Nearby, visit the tomb of ‘Ikrimah bin Abi Jahl, a former enemy turned devout companion of the Prophet , martyred at Yarmouk. Also, explore the revered shrine of Al-Khadir (St. George), associated with divine miracles and cited in the Holy Quran. Return to Amman for dinner and overnight stay.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Amman – The Dead Sea (Prophet Lot) – The Jordan valley-Amman","day_summary":"

After breakfast at your hotel in Amman, drive to the Dead Sea, the lowest point on Earth at 400 meters below sea level. Famous for its high salt and mineral content, the Dead Sea’s waters have been valued for their healing properties since the time of Herod the Great. Enjoy floating effortlessly in its buoyant waters or relax at nearby health spas and luxury resorts. Visit the historic Lot’s Cave, where Prophet Lot took refuge, and see ruins and mosaics linked to his story, mentioned in both the Quran and Bible. Continue to the Jordan Valley to visit the tombs of several revered companions of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH), including Abu Ubeida Amer bin Al-Jarrah, Mo’ath bin Jabal, Shurhabil bin Hasanah, Amir bin Abi Waqqas, and Derar bin Al-Azwar—prominent figures who played key roles in early Islamic history and battles. Return to Amman for dinner and overnight stay.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Amman – Salt (Tombs of Prophet Shu'ayb , prophet Ayyoub , Prophet Yosha , and Maysarah Ben Masrouq Al- 'Abssi –Amman","day_summary":"

After breakfast at your hotel in Amman, drive northwest to Salt, once the capital of Jordan during Ottoman rule and a key administrative center. Wander through its charming streets lined with dazzling houses featuring distinctive long-arched windows, offering a glimpse into its rich past. Visit significant Islamic sites around Salt, including the modern mosque in Wadi Shu’ayb, home to the shrine of Prophet Ju’ayb (Jethro), father-in-law of Prophet Moses, who preached monotheism and honesty to his people. On a hill west of Salt lies the shrine of Prophet Yusha (Joshua), Moses’ successor who led the conquest of Palestine. Nearby, explore the foundations of an ancient building at Kherbit Ayyoub, marking the tomb of Prophet Ayyub (Job), famed for his patience and faith. Also visit the tomb of venerable companion Maysarah bin Masrouq Al-Abssi in ’Arda, who fought in key battles and led the first Muslim army into Byzantine territory. Return to Amman for dinner and overnight.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Amman – Kerak ( battle of Mu’tah ; tombs of Zaid Bin Harithah, Ja`far Bin Abi Talib, Abdullah Bin Ruwahah ,","day_summary":"

After breakfast in Amman, drive south to Kerak to visit the historic site of the Battle of Mutah (629 A.D.), Prophet Muhammad’s fiercest battle against Byzantine and Ghassanid forces. Visit the tombs of revered companions Zaid bin Harithah, Ja’far bin Abi Talib, and Abdullah bin Ruwahah, all martyred in the battle. Zaid, mentioned by name in the Quran, led the army bravely until his death. Ja’far, known as “the flying Ja’far,” lost his hands but held the banner until he fell, and Abdullah, a devoted soldier and poet, also commanded before his martyrdom. Explore Kerak’s shrines, including those of Prophet Nuh (Noah), Prophet Sulayman (Solomon), and Zaid bin Ali bin Al-Hussein, known for their piety and wisdom. End the day visiting Kerak’s famous Crusader castle, notable for its role in battles against Saladin, and nearby Sobak Castle, which fell to Saladin 75 years after its construction.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Free day in Amman (shopping)","day_summary":"

Enjoy breakfast at your hotel in Amman, and start your shopping day. Amman offers various shopping opportunities ranging from gold , spice markets and souqs, to modern boutiques presenting the latest fashions and beautiful local handicrafts. You can find all what you need in malls, like Mecca mall and city mall. You can also enjoy shopping in the old markets of downtown Amman . Evening back to hotel for dinner and overnight .

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Amman – QAIA","day_summary":"

After enjoying breakfast at your hotel, our TravelXploria representative will assist you with your transfer to Queen Alia International Airport (QAIA) for your departure. We ensure a smooth and comfortable journey as you conclude your memorable trip with us, wishing you safe travels and looking forward to welcoming you again.

(Prophet Lot) – The Jordan valley – Amman","day_summary":"

After breakfast at the hotel, begin your day with a drive to the Dead Sea, the lowest point on Earth at 400 meters below sea level. Known for its extreme salinity and rich mineral content, the Dead Sea offers therapeutic benefits recognized since the time of Herod the Great. Enjoy its healing waters and luxurious spa resorts. Nearby, visit the Cave of Prophet Lot, where he sought refuge with his daughters after fleeing the destruction of Sodom. The site aligns with both Islamic and Biblical traditions. Continue to the Jordan Valley to visit the revered Maqamats of several notable Islamic figures. Return to Amman for dinner and overnight stay.


","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Amman – Kerak - Amman","day_summary":"

After breakfast at your hotel in Amman, proceed to Kerak to visit the historic site of the Battle of Mutah, one of the most significant battles fought during the lifetime of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) in 629 A.D. This sacred site honors the bravery of his closest companions—Zaid bin Harithah, Ja’far bin Abi Talib, and Abdullah bin Rawahah—who were martyred in combat against the Byzantine-Ghassanid army. Visit their tombs in Al Mazar near Kerak and learn about their heroic legacies, their role in early Islamic history, and their unwavering dedication to faith. Return to Amman in the afternoon for dinner and overnight stay.



","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Amman – QAIA","day_summary":"

After enjoying breakfast at the hotel, you will be transferred to Queen Alia International Airport in Amman for your departure. Our TravelXploria representative will assist you with all departure formalities, ensuring a smooth and hassle-free end to your journey in Jordan. Safe travels and we hope to see you again!

packages.overview:
• ​Arrival at Queen Alia International Airport, meet TravelXploria representative, transfer to hotel in Amman.
  • Explore Amman’s Citadel and Roman Theatre, followed by a tour of Jordan’s Desert Castles.
  • Visit the ancient Greco-Roman city of Jerash and the hilltop Ajloun Castle.
  • Discover Mount Nebo, Madaba’s mosaics, and hike the Mujib Trail en route to Petra.
  • Guided tour of Petra’s archaeological wonders, then transfer to Wadi Rum for camping.
  • Enjoy a free day in Aqaba by the Red Sea for leisure, snorkeling, or shopping.
  • Transfer from Aqaba to the Dead Sea for relaxation and overnight stay.
  • Breakfast, then transfer to Queen Alia International Airport for departure.

Depart from your hotel and begin your tour of the old city of Amman exploring the Citadel and the Roman Theatre, two of the most popular archaeological sites in the city. Depart from Amman , head East into the desert and visit some of Jordan’s Desert Castles.They are beautiful examples of early Islamic art and architecture, with fine mosaics and frescos. See the stunning artwork in the UNESCO World Heritage Site of Qasr Amra, Al Kharranah palace & Azraq castle, return to Amman. Dinnerand overnight in Amman.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Amman - Jerash – Ajloun – Amman","day_summary":"

After breakfast at the hotel, depart at 08:00 AM for a scenic drive through pine forests and olive groves toward the remarkable city of Jerash. Often called the \"Pompeii of the East,\" Jerash is the most well-preserved Greco-Roman city of the Decapolis, blending Mediterranean and Arab traditions. Once known as Garshu by its early Arab inhabitants, it was renamed Gerasa by the Romans. Visit its colonnaded streets, theaters, and temples. Continue to Ajloun to explore the 12th-century fortress of Qa'lat Al Rabad, built by the forces of Saladin to defend against Crusaders. Return to Amman for dinner and overnight stay.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Petra","country":"Jordan","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Jordan/Petra/1747060757762-AdobeStock_105758945.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Jordan/Petra/1747060758090-AdobeStock_129962293.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Jordan/Petra/1747060758167-AdobeStock_268946672.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Amman - Mount Nebo – Madaba – Mujib trail 2 hours – Petra","day_summary":"

After enjoying a buffet breakfast at the hotel, check out for a full-day excursion. Begin with a visit to Mount Nebo, the revered site where Moses viewed the Promised Land before passing away. Explore ancient church ruins with well-preserved mosaic floors. Continue to Madaba, a town rich in biblical history, and visit St. George’s Church to see the famous 6th-century mosaic map of the Holy Land. Proceed to Wadi Al Mujib for a scenic Ibex Trail hike, then drive along the historic Kings’ Highway and Wadi Al Wala. Finally, travel via the Desert Highway to Petra, check into your hotel, and enjoy dinner and an overnight stay.

Begin your day with a guided exploration of the magnificent ‘Lost City’ of Petra, one of the world’s most extraordinary archaeological sites. Walk through the dramatic Siq, a narrow gorge flanked by towering sandstone cliffs, leading to the stunning Khazneh (Treasury). Discover caves, tombs, and ancient wonders shaped by time and history. Afterward, transfer to the breathtaking desert landscape of Wadi Rum—known as the Valley of the Moon. Explore its vast sandstone mountains, red and white sand dunes, and ancient inscriptions. Follow Lawrence of Arabia’s path, enjoy thrilling desert rides, and experience an unforgettable night under the stars with dinner and overnight camping.

packages.overview:
• ​QAIA – Amman
    Arrival at Queen Alia International Airport, meet & assist, transfer to hotel in Amman for overnight.​
  • ​ Amman – Jerash – Ajloun – Amman
    Explore the ancient Roman city of Jerash and the medieval Ajloun Castle, return to Amman for overnight.
  • Amman – Desert Castles – City Tour – Rainbow Street – Amman
    Visit Jordan’s Desert Castles, tour Amman’s highlights including the Citadel, Roman Theatre & Rainbow Street.
  • Amman – Madaba – Mount Nebo – Dead Sea
    Discover biblical sites in Madaba and Mount Nebo, then float in the healing waters of the Dead Sea.​
  • ​Dead Sea – Petra Visit – Aqaba
    Explore the wonders of Petra including the Treasury and transfer to Aqaba on the Red Sea.​
  • ​ Aqaba – Red Sea Snorkeling – Wadi Rum
    Snorkel in the Red Sea with lunch, enjoy free time in Aqaba, then transfer to Wadi Rum for overnight.​
  • ​Wadi Rum – Amman Shopping – Amman
    Depart Wadi Rum, return to Amman, check in, and enjoy shopping at local malls.​
  • ​ Amman – QAIA
    Breakfast at hotel, then transfer to Queen Alia International Airport for departure.​

Ajloun – Amman","day_summary":"

After breakfast at the hotel, depart at 09:00 AM with your guide through scenic pine forests and olive groves en route to Jerash, one of the best-preserved Greco-Roman cities of the Decapolis, often called the Pompeii of the East. Jerash blends Mediterranean and Arab traditions, with origins dating back to the first millennium BC as Garshu, later Hellenized into Gerasa by the Romans. Explore its impressive ruins and history. Continue to Ajloun to visit the mighty fortress of Qa’lat Al Rabad, strategically perched above the Jordan Valley, from where Saladin’s army launched key battles against the Crusaders. Return to Amman.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Amman – Desert Castles – City tour of Amman – Rainbow Street – Amman","day_summary":"

Buffet breakfast at hotel then check out for full day excursion, depart from your hotel & head East into the desert and visit some of Jordan’s Desert Castles. They are beautiful examples of early Islamic art and architecture, with fine mosaics and frescos. See the stunning artwork in the UNESCO World Heritage Site of Qasr Amra, Al Kharranah palace & Azraq castle. Then Drive back to Amman for city tour the capital of Jordan, includes citadel & Roman theater, and Old Amman, walk in rainbow street - Overnight Amman.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Madaba","country":"Jordan","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Amman – Madaba – Nebo – Dead Sea","day_summary":"

Enjoy a full-day excursion through the historic Mountains of Moab. Begin with a visit to Mount Nebo, just 40 minutes from Amman, where Moses is said to have viewed the Promised Land before his death. Explore the remains of ancient 4th and 6th-century churches, adorned with stunning mosaic floors. Continue to Madaba, only 10 minutes away, known for its biblical history and famous mosaic map at St. George’s Church, depicting Jerusalem, Palestine, and surrounding regions. End the day at the Dead Sea, the lowest point on Earth, where the mineral-rich waters and mud offer renowned therapeutic benefits amid a stark desert landscape.

Breakfast at the hotel in dead sea – then transfer for full day excursion to south to visit Petra so this morning we marvel at the beauty of the ‘Lost City’, exploring its many caves and tombs . Surrounded by imposing mountains and approached through a deep, narrow cleft in the rocks known as the Siq, Petra is a unique and unforgettable place and, enhanced by the play of light and shadow, an altogether overwhelming experience. Approaching the Siq we follow its towering sandstone walls towards the site of the captivating and iconic splendour of the beautiful Khazneh (the famous Treasury), one of the city’s most iconic structures. A guided orientation tour will then take us to some of the key parts of this remarkable and ancient site, one of the most impressive and exciting anywhere in the world , then transfer to Aqaba for overnight .

packages.overview:
• ​Arrival at Queen Alia International Airport and city tour of Amman including the Citadel, Archaeological Museum, and Roman Theater.
  •  Explore Madaba’s mosaic map, Mount Nebo (site of Moses’ tomb), and Mukawer (site of John the Baptist's beheading), then return to Amman.
  •  Visit the well-preserved Roman city of Jerash and the historic Tell Mar Elias site in Ajloun, followed by return to Amman.
  •  Discover the Baptism Site at Bethany, float in the Dead Sea, then travel to Petra for an overnight stay.
  •  Full-day exploration of the rose-red city of Petra, including El Deir (The Monastery) and The Treasury.
  •  Journey through the majestic Wadi Rum desert with a 2-hour jeep tour, then return to Amman.
  •  Breakfast and departure from Queen Alia International Airport.​

Upon your morning arrival at Queen Alia International Airport in Amman, you'll be warmly welcomed by a TravelXploria representative who will assist with visa formalities. Begin your tour of Amman, a city with deep biblical and historical roots. Visit the Citadel, believed to be the site of ancient Rabbath-Ammon, referenced in Deuteronomy and Chronicles. Explore remnants from the Byzantine period, including the Temple of Hercules and an early church. At the Archaeological Museum, view the treasured Dead Sea Scrolls—ancient Hebrew texts discovered in 1952. These invaluable scrolls predate all known biblical texts and speak of hidden treasures in Jordan.

Nebo – Mukawer -Amman","day_summary":"

After breakfast at the hotel, check out and embark on a full-day excursion. Begin with a drive to Madaba, a town rich in biblical history and known for its intricate mosaics. Visit St. George’s Orthodox Church, home to the famous mosaic map depicting Jerusalem, Palestine, Jordan, Israel, and Egypt. Continue to Mount Nebo, the revered site where Moses saw the Promised Land and was later buried nearby. Explore the remains of ancient churches with beautifully preserved mosaic floors. Then head to Mukawer, where ruins of a Byzantine church overlook the site of Herod’s fortress—where John the Baptist was beheaded. Return to Amman.


","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Amman – Jerash-Ajloun-Amman","day_summary":"

After breakfast, drive north to explore Jerash, one of the best-preserved Roman cities in the Near East, once known as Antioch on the Chrysorhoas. Founded in the 2nd century BC, it thrived under Roman rule and became part of the Decapolis. Highlights include the Temple of Artemis, the Cardo, the North Theatre, and Byzantine-era churches. Continue to Ajloun to visit Tell Mar Elias, believed to be the hometown of Prophet Elijah. The site holds remains of one of Jordan’s largest Byzantine churches. Artifacts from the site are housed in the Ajloun Castle Museum. Return to Amman for dinner and overnight.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Petra","country":"Jordan","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Jordan/Petra/1747060757762-AdobeStock_105758945.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Jordan/Petra/1747060758090-AdobeStock_129962293.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Jordan/Petra/1747060758167-AdobeStock_268946672.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Amman – Bethany - Dead Sea – Petra","day_summary":"

After breakfast in Amman, visit the Baptism Site at Bethany Beyond the Jordan, where John the Baptist baptized Jesus, marking the start of His ministry (Luke 3:21–23). This sacred place also witnessed the calling of Jesus’ first disciples and His early prayers. Then, continue to the Dead Sea, Earth’s lowest point at 400 meters below sea level. Known for its healing minerals and salt-rich waters, the Dead Sea offers a unique experience, with therapeutic mud and warm, buoyant water. Enjoy the breathtaking sunset views. In the evening, transfer to Petra for dinner and overnight stay, concluding a spiritually rich day.

After breakfast at the hotel, begin a full-day exploration of the rose-red city of Petra at 8:00 AM. This ancient marvel, carved into vibrant sandstone cliffs, holds immense historical and biblical significance. Inhabited since prehistoric times, Petra was home to the Edomites and later flourished under the Nabateans. The city's breathtaking monuments, like the Treasury (El Khazneh) and the Monastery (El Deir), are architectural wonders. El Deir, larger and more remote, offers sweeping views of Wadi Mousa and Wadi Araba, with the Tomb of Aaron visible in the distance. Petra's charm lies in its rich textures, mysterious past, and poetic beauty—earning its title as a \"rose-red city half as old as time.\" Return to your hotel in Petra for dinner and overnight.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Amman","country":"Jordan","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Jordan/Amman/1744968605737-AdobeStock_129962293.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Jordan/Amman/1744968606249-AdobeStock_190604000.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Jordan/Amman/1744968606276-AdobeStock_259086143.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Petra – Wadi Rum (2-hour jeep tour) – Amman","day_summary":"

After breakfast at your hotel in Petra, drive to the majestic Wadi Rum, famously known as the Valley of the Moon. With its vast desert landscape, vibrant colors, and towering rock formations, Wadi Rum feels more like another planet than Earth. T.E. Lawrence, who revered its grandeur, described it as “landscapes in childhood’s dreams, vast and silent.” The silence, scale, and natural beauty inspire awe and humility. The longer one stays, the deeper the connection grows, making departure bittersweet. This surreal desert leaves a lasting impression on every traveler. In the evening, transfer to Amman for dinner and an overnight stay.

Visit iconic landmarks including the Citadel, Temple of Hercules, Roman Theatre, Nymphaeum, King Abdullah Mosque (Blue Mosque), and the Umayyad Palace. Jerash-Ajloun-Amman","day_summary":"

After breakfast at the hotel, begin your exploration of Amman, the capital of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, once known as Rabbat Ammon and later as Philadelphia. Visit key highlights including the Citadel, Temple of Hercules, Roman Theatre, Nymphaeum, King Abdullah Mosque (Blue Mosque), and the Umayyad Palace. Continue through scenic pine forests and olive groves to Jerash, the best-preserved Greco-Roman city of the Decapolis, often called the Pompeii of the East. Discover its rich history blending Roman and Arab influences. Then, travel to Ajloun to visit the impressive Qa'lat Al Rabad fortress, a strategic base for Saladin’s campaigns. Return to Amman for overnight stay.

Here there are ruins of 4th. and 6th c. churches whose floors are still covered with marvelous mosaic. Afterwards to Madaba this town has a long history, being first mentioned in the Bible and in the Mesha Stele when it was a Moabite town, visit St. George orthodox Christian church the mosaic Map of Jerusalem, Palestine, Jordan, Israel & Egypt. Madaba is famous as a land of mosaics! Then you will be transferred to Petra the Nabatean rose-red city& one of theworld’s seven wonders. Dinner and overnight at the hotel.

Discover its most iconic monuments carved into rose-red rock, including the world-famous Treasury—featured in the Indiana Jones films—the Royal Tombs, and the Colored Tombs. Petra is truly one of the must-visit wonders of the world. After the visit, depart for Wadi Rum, the majestic desert once traversed by Lawrence of Arabia. Embark on a thrilling 2-hour 4x4 Jeep tour led by local Bedouins through the striking lunar landscape, where granite massifs and pink sands create surreal natural formations. Continue to Aqaba for dinner and overnight accommodation.

Start your day with breakfast at your hotel in Aqaba, a beautiful coastal city on the Red Sea known for its beaches and vibrant water activities. Depart for Petra, where you will enjoy breakfast before beginning your unforgettable exploration of the Nabatean rose-red city. Upon arrival at the Visitor Center, take a 20-minute horseback ride to the Siq, followed by a guided tour through the canyon to the iconic Treasury. Spend the day exploring Petra’s wonders including the City Center, Royal Tombs, and Theater. Later, visit Little Petra, a quieter site showcasing exquisite Nabataean rock-cut architecture. Return to your hotel, enjoy a buffet dinner, and overnight in Petra.

Experience Jordan’s top historical and cultural highlights on this unforgettable journey. Explore Petra, one of the Seven Wonders of the World, marvel at Byzantine mosaics, and enjoy a thrilling 4x4 Jeep tour in Wadi Rum. Relax at the Dead Sea and spend a magical night in a desert camp


  • .Mount Nebo –The memorial place of Moses
  • Madaba – St. George’s Church and the mosaic map of the Holy Land.
  • Little Petra - Nabatean caravan station
  • Petra – the spectacular Rose- Red city of the ancient Nabateans and one of the world’s seven
    wonders (Petra)
  • Wadi Rum – the home of Lawrence of Arabia, also known as the valley of the moon
  • Dead Sea – unique swimming experience in buoyant water at the lowest point on the Earth. Take
    advantage of the useful Dead Sea rich minerals and mud.

packages.overview:
Join our interesting tour & spend your holidayin Jordan, an ideal destination for travelers, this
vacation is recommended to those who want to have a memorable tour within a short time.

  • Mount Nebo –The memorialplace of Moses
  • Madaba – St. George’sChurch and the mosaic map of the Holy Land.
  • Kerak – The imposingCrusader castle.
  • Wadi Rum – the home of LawrenceOf Arabia, also known as the valley of the moon
  • Petra – the spectacular Rose- Red city of the ancient Nabateansand one of the world’s seven
    wonders (Petra).

After breakfast, meet at the hotel in Amman and depart for a full-day historical excursion through the majestic Mountains of Moab. Begin your journey in Madaba, an ancient town mentioned in the Bible and the Mesha Stele. Visit St. George’s Orthodox Church, renowned for its stunning mosaic map depicting Jerusalem, Palestine, Jordan, Israel, and Egypt. Proceed to Mount Nebo, the revered site where Moses viewed the Promised Land and was later buried. Explore the ruins of ancient churches adorned with remarkable mosaics. Continue your drive along the scenic King's Highway, passing Wadi Al Wala and Dhiban, known for the famous Moabite Stone. Then head to Kerak Castle, an impressive Crusader fortress featuring massive stone walls, vaulted galleries, and a deep defensive moat. Conclude your day with a transfer via the desert highway to Wadi Rum. Upon arrival, check in at the camp, enjoy dinner, and overnight stay under the stars.

overview:
Best Jordan Tour Packages from India – Explore the Wonders of the Middle East
Welcome to Jordan, a land where stories are etched in stone, deserts whisper secrets of ancient prophets, and natural wonders soothe the soul. With Travelxploria—your best travel agency, trusted best international tour operator, and one of the best tours and travel agency in India—explore Jordan through captivating tour packages crafted for explorers, families, and honeymooners alike. Backed by over a decade of experience and expert local guides, Travelxploria ensures you experience Jordan authentically and comfortably. Whether you’re booking Jordan tour packages from Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, or beyond, our tailored itineraries guarantee an unforgettable journey.
For a broader set of destinations, also explore our wide range of Tour Package India.

Why Choose Jordan for Your Next International Trip from India?

Ancient History and World Heritage Sites
Jordan boasts an extraordinary collection of ancient history and UNESCO World Heritage Sites. Your trip begins in Amman, where modern cafes nestle beside Roman ruins like the Citadel and the majestic Roman Amphitheatre. Explore the mosaics of Madaba, including the famous Madaba Map, and stand at Mount Nebo, where Moses is said to have viewed the Promised Land. A visit to Petra—the rose-red city and one of the New Seven Wonders of the World—is a highlight in every Jordan tour package.
(For the latest site timings and visitor guidelines, we recommend checking official tourism resources before travel.)
These destinations offer deep cultural and spiritual experiences that families, culture lovers, and history enthusiasts cherish. Travelxploria, among the best tour and travel company in India, ensures you don't miss a thing.

Unique Desert Landscapes and Red Sea Beaches
Beyond the cities, Jordan’s desert landscapes and seaside retreats await. Experience the otherworldly beauty of Wadi Rum’s towering rock formations and red sand dunes on thrilling jeep safaris, stargazing nights, and authentic Bedouin dinners.
(Tip: Consider a hot air balloon ride over Wadi Rum for a breathtaking perspective.)
